data.body: PROBLEM RECORD (for the bug that links here). During execution of epic bf50 (graceless-hominine-jerboa), every engineering acceptance criterion across all 19 stories was completed, reviewed, and LANDED on main (Gerrit 537-555 + 559, each LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1, MERGED). 18/19 children earned BOTH signatures (plan-review + completion-verifier) and closed SIGNED. The 19th, 5f39, carried one [operator-attested] acceptance criterion (AC3) whose admissible evidence lives entirely OUTSIDE the codebase: configured GitHub repo VARIABLES (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_PROJECT, ACLI_VERSION/ACLI_SHA256), a configured SECRET (JIRA_API_TOKEN), a provisioned dedicated disposable Jira test project, and the URL of a green workflow_dispatch canary run in which the live Jira test EXECUTED (not all-skip). The executing agent had neither the access nor the authorization to produce any of that evidence, so AC3 could not be attested. Consequence: 5f39's completion-verifier FAILED on that single criterion (all its CODE criteria passed once the change landed), so 5f39 could only be force-closed UNSIGNED; and because epic bf50's own AC requires 'all child stories closed (SIGNED)', the epic likewise could not earn a completion-verifier signature and was force-closed UNSIGNED. Net: an epic whose entire engineering scope was demonstrably complete and landed cannot be represented in the store as certified/signed, purely because one child's evidence is out-of-band from the agent's reach. This is a durable process/tooling gap, not a defect in any of the 19 code changes.
